title: Best VPN for Brazil 2026: Top Picks for Privacy, Streaming and Speed
description: The best VPN for Brazil in 2026: NordVPN for speed, ExpressVPN for streaming Globoplay from abroad, Mullvad for privacy. Covers ISP throttling, LGPD, and Brazilian streaming access.
content:
The best VPN for Brazil in 2026 is NordVPN for most users, ExpressVPN for accessing Globoplay and Brazilian content from abroad, and Mullvad for users who need maximum privacy. Brazil has specific internet conditions worth knowing: ISP throttling during peak hours, ANATEL bandwidth regulations, the Lei Geral de Proteção de Dados (LGPD) data-sharing framework, and geo-blocked streaming content make a VPN a practical tool for millions of Brazilian internet users.

Why You Need a VPN in Brazil

Brazil is one of the largest internet markets in the world, with over 180 million internet users. The internet environment is mostly open compared to countries like China or the UAE, but specific practical issues make a VPN useful for everyday Brazilians and for the Brazilian diaspora abroad.

ISP throttling: Brazilian mobile operators and broadband providers including Claro, Vivo (Telefónica), TIM, and Oi have been documented throttling video streaming traffic during congested hours. ANATEL, the national telecommunications regulator, requires ISPs to disclose traffic management practices but does not prohibit throttling. A VPN encrypts your traffic so your ISP cannot identify video streams and apply differential bandwidth limits.

LGPD and surveillance concerns: Brazil's Lei Geral de Proteção de Dados (LGPD) came into full effect in 2021. It is modeled after the EU's GDPR and gives users rights over their personal data. However, the Marco Civil da Internet requires ISPs to retain connection logs for 12 months and application providers to retain 6 months of access records. These logs can be requested by courts. A VPN shifts the logging question from your ISP to your VPN provider — choosing a no-logs provider outside Brazil removes Brazilian ISP logs from the equation.

Public Wi-Fi security: Brazilian airports, shopping malls (shoppings), and cafés offer free Wi-Fi, but security standards vary widely. Man-in-the-middle attacks on unsecured public networks are a documented risk. A VPN encrypts your traffic before it leaves your device, protecting your login credentials, banking sessions, and browsing from other users on the same network.

Accessing Brazilian content from abroad: Globoplay, the streaming platform from Rede Globo, is one of the most popular services in Brazil. It carries Brazilian football (Campeonato Brasileiro, Copa do Brasil), Brazilian novelas, and Globo originals. From outside Brazil, Globoplay requires a Brazilian IP address for most content. Brazilian expatriates in Portugal, the US, Japan, and elsewhere use VPNs to maintain access. The same applies to SporTV, Premiere FC, and Record Play.

Accessing international content from Brazil: Some international streaming libraries are smaller in Brazil than in the US or Europe due to regional licensing. Netflix Brazil carries a smaller library than Netflix US. A VPN set to a US server gives access to the full Netflix US catalog. The same applies to Prime Video, Disney+, and niche streaming services with larger English-language libraries.

Best VPN Providers for Brazil (2026)

1. NordVPN (Best Overall)

NordVPN has servers in Brazil (São Paulo) and offers fast connections via the NordLynx protocol, built on WireGuard. For Brazilian users connecting to local servers, latency is typically under 15 milliseconds on fiber connections in São Paulo and Rio de Janeiro. For international connections, NordVPN's extensive server network across the US and Europe offers the lowest latency for Brazilian users needing to bypass geo-restrictions.

NordVPN holds independent no-logs audits from PricewaterhouseCoopers (2023) and Deloitte (2024). Its servers are RAM-only, meaning no data is written to persistent storage. If law enforcement seizes a server, there is no user data to extract. Threat Protection blocks ads, malware domains, and trackers at the DNS level. Price: from 3.09 USD per month on a two-year plan. Up to ten simultaneous device connections. Best overall choice for most Brazilian users and for Brazilians abroad accessing Globoplay.

2. ExpressVPN (Best for Globoplay from Abroad)

ExpressVPN has servers in Brazil (São Paulo) and is the most consistent performer for accessing Globoplay and other Brazilian streaming platforms from outside Brazil. Its Lightway protocol offers fast reconnections, which matters when switching between content that may trigger geo-detection. MediaStreamer Smart DNS lets Brazilian expats access Globoplay on Smart TVs and streaming sticks that cannot run a VPN app natively.

ExpressVPN is based in the British Virgin Islands, outside Brazil's LGPD jurisdiction. Its no-logs policy has been verified through an independent audit by PwC and confirmed in a 2017 incident where Turkish authorities seized an ExpressVPN server and found no useful user data. Priced higher at 6.67 USD per month on an annual plan. Worth the cost if Globoplay football coverage or novela access from abroad is the primary use case.

3. Mullvad (Best for Privacy)

Mullvad requires no email address to sign up, uses randomly generated account numbers, and accepts cash by mail, Monero, and Bitcoin as payment. In 2023, Swedish police raided Mullvad's offices. They left empty-handed because Mullvad had no user logs, no connection records, and no account details tied to real identities. This is a no-logs policy proven in practice, not just written in a privacy policy.

Mullvad has Brazilian servers (São Paulo) and offers WireGuard with DAITA (Defence Against AI-guided Traffic Analysis), which resists sophisticated traffic correlation attacks. For journalists, activists, lawyers handling sensitive cases, or anyone with a serious privacy threat model, Mullvad is the right choice. Price: 5 EUR per month flat, no annual lock-in required.

4. Surfshark (Best Budget Option)

Surfshark offers Brazil servers (São Paulo) with no device connection limit, making it the best value for households or shared subscriptions. Price: 2.19 USD per month on a two-year plan. Nexus routing distributes your traffic through multiple nodes for improved anonymity. CleanWeb blocks ads and phishing domains. Average speeds on Brazilian servers: 130 to 190 Mbps on a 300 Mbps fiber connection. Works reliably with Netflix US from Brazil and with Globoplay from abroad.

5. Proton VPN (Best Free Option)

Proton VPN is based in Switzerland, outside LGPD's jurisdiction and independent of any Brazilian court order. Its free tier has no data cap and no speed throttling on free servers. The free tier does not include Brazilian servers, but paid plans (from 4 EUR per month) include São Paulo. Proton's Stealth protocol disguises VPN traffic as HTTPS, which helps in environments where ISPs use deep packet inspection to throttle or block VPN protocols. Proton publishes transparency reports and a Warrant Canary and has never handed user data to law enforcement.

Brazilian Internet Regulations: What They Mean for VPN Users

Brazil's internet legal framework has several layers relevant to VPN users.

Marco Civil da Internet (2014): Brazil's

established net neutrality, required ISPs to retain connection logs for 12 months, and required application providers to retain 6 months of user access records. Courts can demand these logs via a judicial order. A VPN shifts the logging point from your ISP (who logs everything about your connection) to your VPN provider (who, if you chose well, logs nothing).

LGPD (2020): The Lei Geral de Proteção de Dados is Brazil's main data protection law, enforced by ANPD (Autoridade Nacional de Proteção de Dados). It gives users rights to access, correct, and delete their data held by companies. It applies to companies operating in Brazil or processing data of people in Brazil. Your VPN provider's LGPD obligations depend on whether they have any establishment in Brazil. Providers like Mullvad (Sweden), Proton (Switzerland), and NordVPN (Panama) operate outside LGPD's primary jurisdiction.

ANATEL and ISP throttling: Brazil's telecom regulator allows ISPs to manage traffic during congestion but requires them to disclose their practices. In practice, Brazilian ISPs throttle mobile data after plan caps are exhausted and apply video throttling during peak hours on fixed broadband. ANATEL's transparency requirements mean throttling must be disclosed, but not prohibited. A VPN bypasses application-layer throttling regardless of what ANATEL permits.

How to Access Globoplay from Outside Brazil

Globoplay is the streaming platform of TV Globo, Brazil's dominant free-to-air broadcaster. It carries live TV, football matches (Campeonato Brasileiro Série A, Copa do Brasil, state championships), Brazilian novelas (novelas), Globo originals, and HBO Max content licensed for Brazil.

From outside Brazil, Globoplay requires a Brazilian IP address. Here is how to access it:

  1. Subscribe to a VPN with Brazilian servers. NordVPN and ExpressVPN are the most reliable choices as of July 2026.
  2. Connect to a Brazilian server, specifically São Paulo. São Paulo servers have the most capacity and the lowest latency for users in the Americas, Europe, and Japan.
  3. Open Globoplay at globoplay.globo.com or in the mobile app. The app checks your IP on connection, so the VPN must be connected before you open it.
  4. If the site shows a geo-block error, disconnect from the VPN, clear your browser cookies, reconnect to a different São Paulo server, and reload.
  5. For smart TVs without native VPN app support, use ExpressVPN's MediaStreamer Smart DNS. Configure the DNS servers on your TV's network settings — no VPN app required.

Note: Globoplay has different subscription tiers. The free tier (with ads) includes some content. Globoplay Mais and Globoplay + Canais packages require a Brazilian payment method. Some content on the paid tiers requires a Brazilian credit card. The free tier is accessible with just a Brazilian email address and VPN connection.

Accessing International Streaming from Brazil

Brazil's Netflix library is significantly smaller than the US catalog. In mid-2026, Netflix Brazil carries approximately 3,800 titles versus over 6,000 on Netflix US. The difference is driven by regional licensing agreements: studios and distributors sell streaming rights by territory, and some rights for Brazil go to local broadcasters (Globo, RecordPlay, SBT) rather than Netflix.

With a VPN set to a US server, a Brazilian subscriber gets access to the Netflix US library. The subscription cost remains the same — the VPN changes your apparent location, not your billing country. The same approach works for Prime Video US (more English-language exclusives) and Hulu (US-only).

For speed, connect to a US server geographically close to you. For users in São Paulo, servers in Miami or New York City typically offer 60 to 90 millisecond latency on fiber broadband. NordVPN's Miami servers and ExpressVPN's New York servers are the fastest options for Brazilian users connecting to US content.

Is a VPN Legal in Brazil?

Yes. Using a VPN is fully legal in Brazil for personal and business use. There is no law prohibiting encrypted connections or VPN software. The Marco Civil da Internet explicitly protects privacy as a user right and endorses encryption as a privacy tool.

Using a VPN to access geo-restricted content may violate a streaming platform's terms of service. This is a contractual matter between you and the platform, not a criminal one. Platforms can terminate accounts that violate their terms, but there is no Brazilian law penalizing users for accessing geo-restricted content. VPNs for privacy, security, and remote work are straightforwardly legal.

VPN Speeds in Brazil: What to Expect

Brazilian broadband infrastructure has improved significantly, with fiber-to-the-home (FTTH) coverage exceeding 60% in major cities. Average fixed broadband speeds in Brazil are around 130 Mbps as of 2026. Mobile speeds on 4G/5G networks average 35 to 80 Mbps in cities.

A VPN adds overhead from encryption and routing through a VPN server. On fiber connections above 100 Mbps, expect 10 to 20% speed reduction when connecting to a local Brazilian VPN server. Connecting to international servers (US, Europe) adds latency proportional to physical distance: São Paulo to Miami is roughly 70 to 90 ms base latency. For streaming, this is imperceptible. For real-time gaming, it matters more.

WireGuard-based protocols (NordLynx, Surfshark WireGuard) consistently outperform older protocols like OpenVPN on high-bandwidth connections. For downloading or 4K streaming, use WireGuard on whichever provider you choose.

Free vs Paid VPN for Brazil

Free VPNs come with limitations that matter specifically for Brazilian users:

  • No Brazilian servers on free tiers: Most free VPN tiers exclude Brazil. For accessing Globoplay from abroad or for local privacy, a free tier without Brazilian servers is useless for the primary use cases.
  • Data caps: Free tiers typically cap at 500 MB to 2 GB per month. A single football match on Globoplay at 1080p uses 4 to 6 GB. Data caps make free VPNs impractical for streaming.
  • Privacy risks: Several free VPN apps have been caught selling user data or injecting ads into traffic. In Brazil's data environment, a logging free VPN may be worse than no VPN — your ISP logs are replaced by your VPN provider's logs, which are sold to advertisers.

Proton VPN Free is the exception for privacy: no data cap, no logs, Swiss jurisdiction. For streaming use, a paid plan at 2 to 5 USD per month is practical. Surfshark at 2.19 USD per month is the best value entry point for Brazilian users.

How to Set Up a VPN in Brazil

Setup takes under five minutes on any device:

  1. Choose a provider. For most users: NordVPN. For Globoplay access from abroad: ExpressVPN. For maximum privacy: Mullvad.
  2. Download the app from the provider's official website (not from third-party app stores or APK sites).
  3. Create an account and select a plan. Mullvad accepts Boleto Bancário via Monero exchange if you want to pay without a credit card linked to your identity.
  4. Open the app. To access Globoplay from abroad: connect to a São Paulo server. To access US Netflix from Brazil: connect to a US server (Miami or New York for lowest latency).
  5. Verify the connection at ipleak.net or dnsleaktest.com. Confirm your displayed IP address is in the country you selected.
  6. Enable the kill switch in app settings. This blocks all internet traffic if the VPN connection drops, preventing your real IP from being briefly exposed.

Frequently Asked Questions

What is the best VPN for Brazil in 2026?

NordVPN is the best overall VPN for Brazil in 2026, with São Paulo servers, a third-party audited no-logs policy, and fast NordLynx speeds. For accessing Globoplay from outside Brazil, ExpressVPN is the most reliable option. For maximum privacy, Mullvad.

Can I watch Globoplay from outside Brazil with a VPN?

Yes. Connect to a VPN Brazil server (São Paulo) before opening Globoplay. NordVPN and ExpressVPN are the most consistent options as of July 2026. The VPN must be connected before you launch the app or website. If you see a geo-block error, clear cookies, switch to a different São Paulo server, and reload.

Is using a VPN legal in Brazil?

Yes, VPNs are fully legal in Brazil for personal and business use. The Marco Civil da Internet protects privacy and encryption as user rights. Using a VPN to access geo-restricted content may violate a platform's terms of service but is not illegal under Brazilian law.

Does a VPN help with ISP throttling in Brazil?

Yes. Brazilian ISPs including Claro, Vivo, TIM, and Oi throttle video streaming traffic during peak hours. A VPN encrypts all traffic, preventing your ISP from identifying and throttling video streams. Users with throttled connections typically see measurable streaming quality improvements when using a VPN during 7 PM to 11 PM peak hours.

Which VPN is best for accessing Netflix US from Brazil?

NordVPN and ExpressVPN consistently unblock Netflix US from Brazilian connections. Connect to a US server (Miami or New York) before opening Netflix. The Netflix US library has roughly 2,000 more titles than the Brazilian catalog. Your subscription billing remains in Brazil.

Does Brazil have VPN restrictions?

No. Brazil does not block VPN traffic and does not restrict VPN use. VPN protocols including WireGuard, OpenVPN, and IKEv2 operate freely on Brazilian ISPs. This makes Brazil's VPN environment significantly more open than countries like China, Russia, or the UAE, where VPN protocols face active blocking.

What is LGPD and does it affect my VPN use?

LGPD is Brazil's Lei Geral de Proteção de Dados, Brazil's data protection law similar to Europe's GDPR. It gives you rights over your personal data held by companies. Using a VPN provider based outside Brazil (like NordVPN in Panama or Mullvad in Sweden) means your VPN usage data is subject to that country's laws, not LGPD. Most privacy-focused VPN providers retain no user data regardless of jurisdiction.
